ABOUT US
CONTACT US

Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2026

At Octaloop Technologies Private Limited ("Octaloop," "we," "our," or "us"), we are committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, share, and safeguard your information when you visit our website octaloop.com, attend our events (including India Blockchain Tour, India AI Tour, and Metamorphosis), use our marketing services, or interact with us in any capacity (collectively, the "Services").

By accessing or using our Services, you consent to the practices described in this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree with this policy, please do not use our Services.

1. Information We Collect

We collect information in the following ways:

1.1 Information You Provide Directly

  • Contact Information: Name, email address, phone number, company name, job title, and city when you fill out forms on our website, register for events, purchase tickets, submit sponsorship inquiries, or subscribe to our newsletters.
  • Event Registration Data: Information provided during event registration including professional background, areas of interest, dietary preferences, and any accessibility requirements.
  • Sponsorship and Partnership Inquiries: Company details, marketing objectives, budget indications, and other business information shared during partnership discussions.
  • Speaker and Participant Submissions: Bios, headshots, presentation materials, and professional credentials submitted by speakers, panelists, and workshop facilitators.
  • Communications: Any messages, feedback, or inquiries you send us via email, contact forms, social media, or other communication channels.
  • Payment Information: Billing details provided when purchasing event tickets or sponsorship packages. We do not store credit card or banking details directly — all payments are processed through PCI-DSS compliant third-party processors (such as Razorpay and Stripe).

1.2 Information Collected Automatically

  • Usage Data: Pages visited, time spent on pages, click patterns, referral sources, and navigation paths collected through analytics tools when you visit our website.
  • Device and Browser Information: Browser type and version, operating system, device type, screen resolution, and language preferences.
  • IP Address and Location Data: Your IP address and approximate geographic location derived from it.
  • Cookies and Tracking Technologies: Data collected through cookies, web beacons, pixels, and similar technologies as described in Section 5 below.

1.3 Information from Third Parties

We may receive information about you from third-party platforms including LinkedIn, Eventbrite, Luma, Telegram, Discord, and other platforms where you interact with our content or register for our events. We may also receive information from our event sponsors and partners where you have consented to share your data with them.

2. How We Use Your Information

  • Service Delivery: To process event registrations, issue tickets, manage attendee logistics, and deliver our marketing and consulting services to clients.
  • Communications: To respond to your inquiries, send event confirmations, share updates about upcoming events, and deliver newsletters and marketing communications (with your consent).
  • Event Operations: To manage on-ground event logistics including check-in, badge printing, session management, and attendee experience optimization across all cities on the tour.
  • Sponsor and Partner Deliverables: To fulfill our obligations to event sponsors and partners, which may include sharing aggregated and anonymized attendee data, post-event reports, and qualified lead information where attendees have consented to such sharing.
  • Website Improvement: To analyze website traffic and usage patterns, optimize content and user experience, and troubleshoot technical issues.
  • Business Development: To manage our sponsorship and partnership pipeline, communicate with prospective and existing sponsors, and improve our service offerings.
  • Legal and Compliance: To comply with applicable laws, regulations, and legal processes, and to protect our rights, property, and safety, and that of our users, sponsors, and partners.

3. How We Share Your Information

We do not sell your personal information to third parties. We may share your information in the following circumstances:

  • Event Sponsors and Partners: Where you attend a sponsored session, visit a sponsor booth, participate in a sponsor-led workshop or hackathon, or otherwise interact with a sponsor's activation at our events, your registration information (name, email, company, job title) may be shared with that sponsor. We will make reasonable efforts to inform you at the point of interaction.
  • Service Providers: We share information with third-party service providers who assist us in operating our website, processing payments, sending communications, managing event logistics, and analyzing data. These providers are contractually obligated to use your information solely for the purposes of providing services to us.
  • Legal Requirements: We may disclose your information if required to do so by law, court order, or government regulation, or if we believe in good faith that such disclosure is necessary to protect our rights, protect your safety or the safety of others, investigate fraud, or respond to a government request.
  • Business Transfers: In the event of a merger, acquisition, reorganization, or sale of all or a portion of our assets, your information may be transferred as part of that transaction.

4. Data Retention

We retain your personal information for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected, comply with our legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements. Event registration data is retained for a period of three years following the event, after which it is anonymized or deleted. Newsletter subscriber data is retained until you unsubscribe. Sponsorship and client data is retained for the duration of the business relationship and for five years thereafter.

5. Cookies and Tracking Technologies

We use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your experience on our website. These include:

  • Essential Cookies: Required for the website to function properly, including session management and security features.
  • Analytics Cookies: Used to collect anonymized data about how visitors use our website, including pages visited, time spent, and navigation paths. We use Google Analytics and similar tools for this purpose.
  • Marketing Cookies: Used to track visitors across websites for the purpose of displaying relevant advertisements and measuring campaign effectiveness.

You can control cookie settings through your browser preferences. Disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality of our website. By continuing to use our website without adjusting your cookie settings, you consent to our use of cookies as described here.

6. Data Security

We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include encrypted data transmission (SSL/TLS), access controls, secure data storage, regular security assessments, and employee training on data protection practices. However, no method of transmission over the internet or electronic storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

7. International Data Transfers

As we operate events across multiple cities in India and work with international sponsors and partners, your information may be transferred to and processed in jurisdictions outside your country of residence. Where such transfers occur, we take reasonable steps to ensure that your information receives an adequate level of protection consistent with applicable data protection laws.

8. Your Rights

Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal information:

  • Right of Access: You may request a copy of the personal information we hold about you.
  • Right to Correction: You may request that we correct any inaccurate or incomplete information.
  • Right to Deletion: You may request that we delete your personal information, subject to certain legal exceptions.
  • Right to Withdraw Consent: Where we process your information based on your consent, you may withdraw that consent at any time. This will not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal.
  • Right to Opt Out: You may unsubscribe from our marketing communications at any time by clicking the unsubscribe link in our emails or contacting us directly.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at contact@octaloop.com. We will respond to your request within 30 days.

9. Children's Privacy

Our Services are not directed to individuals under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that we have inadvertently collected information from a child under 18, we will take steps to delete such information promptly.

10. Third-Party Links

Our website and communications may contain links to third-party websites, services, and platforms. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of these third parties. We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party sites you visit.

11. Changes to This Privacy Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, legal requirements, or business operations. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last updated" date. Your continued use of our Services following the posting of changes constitutes your acceptance of those changes.

12. Contact Us

If you have questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at:

Octaloop Technologies Private Limited
Email: contact@octaloop.com
Website: octaloop.com
Location: New Delhi, India
